sentence_reviews.text: GRPO is a PPO-style policy update that estimates relative advantages from groups of sampled responses instead of learning a separate value model.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: DeepSeekMath introduces GRPO for mathematical reasoning RL and describes estimating advantages from grouped outputs without a learned critic/value model; RLHF/RL references support policy-gradient context.
sentence_reviews.notes: DeepSeekMath directly presents GRPO as a PPO-like method estimating group-relative advantages without a separate value model.

sentence_reviews.text: It reduces critic-memory overhead and fits reasoning tasks where several candidate completions can share one verifiable reward rule.

edge_reviews.notes: Policy-gradient support is clear; actor-critic as core is weakened because GRPO removes the critic/value model.
GRPO fits RLHF/post-training optimization families, though this is a taxonomic synthesis.
GRPO is commonly paired with verifiable rewards for reasoning; common strength is reasonable.
SFT initialization is common in the cited post-training pipeline.
